Supplementary Analysis: Excitatory Neuron Classification in Postnatal PsychAD Cells

Root-cause evaluation of low EN fraction in scANVI-integrated infant transcriptomes

Author

R.A. Dear

Published

May 25, 2026

Background

In the integrated snRNAseq dataset combining Wang (2022), Velmeshev (2022; hereafter Vel), and PsychAD (Mathys 2023), scANVI-based label transfer assigns approximately 5% of PsychAD infant (<1 year) cells to the Excitatory (EN) class. By contrast, Wang and Vel cells from the same developmental window carry EN fractions of ~25% and ~30%, respectively. Given that the prefrontal cortex is characterised by substantial excitatory neuron populations at birth (in utero EN migration is largely complete by mid-gestation), a 5-fold deficit raises the question of whether the low EN fraction reflects (i) genuine biological differences in the PsychAD cohort, (ii) a technical or normalisation artefact, or (iii) a misclassification error arising from the integration model.

This supplementary analysis applies a series of orthogonal marker-based diagnostics to the raw, unnormalised count matrices of each source. The principal conclusion is that the EN fraction in PsychAD infants is genuinely low relative to Wang/Vel — partly reflecting a biological signature of early postnatal EN immaturity — but is further suppressed by a label-transfer artefact caused by the dominance of 453,000 adult PsychAD cells in the scANVI supervision signal.

1 Hypothesis H9 Rejected: Library Depth and HVG Selection Are Not Confounders

1.1 Per-cell library depth

A straightforward confound would arise if PsychAD cells have systematically lower UMI counts than Wang/Vel, causing EN markers to fall below detection thresholds under CPM normalisation. We examined total UMI depth for the matched <1 year subsets from each source.

Show code 
qc <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "hvg_intersection_v2", "per_cell_qc.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD" = "PsychAD",
      "WANG"    = "Wang",
      "VEL"     = "Vel"
    ),
    group_label = factor(group_label, levels = c("PsychAD", "Wang", "Vel"))
  )

p_depth <- ggplot(qc, aes(x = group_label, fill = group_label)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = total_counts_median), colour = "white", width = 0.55) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = total_counts_P25, ymax = total_counts_P75),
    width = 0.22, linewidth = 0.6, colour = "grey30"
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(y = total_counts_mean), shape = 21, fill = "white",
             colour = "grey20", size = 2.5, stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_comma(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "UMI count per cell",
    title = "Library depth: PsychAD is ~2× deeper than Wang/Vel"
  ) +
  theme_pub()

p_depth

PsychAD infant cells have a median of 8,636 UMI/cell (mean 12,587), compared with 5,486 (mean 6,508) for Wang and 4,319 (mean 5,560) for Vel. Because CPM normalisation scales expression inversely with library size, deeper PsychAD libraries would, if anything, reduce normalised marker counts per unit of genuine expression — making this hypothesis untenable in the direction that would inflate IN relative to EN.

1.2 EN:IN ratio is invariant across gene-set sizes

A second technical concern is that HVG selection could preferentially capture IN markers while excluding EN markers when Wang/Vel gene space is used as the reference. We evaluated the EN:IN marker ratio across HVG intersection sizes ranging from 423 to 15,000 genes.

Show code 
hvg <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "hvg_intersection_v2", "markers_by_hvg_size.csv"),
                show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  filter(intersection_size >= 1000) |>   # ≥1000 genes: all EN markers captured
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD" = "PsychAD",
      "WANG"    = "Wang",
      "VEL"     = "Vel"
    ),
    group_label = factor(group_label, levels = c("PsychAD", "Wang", "Vel"))
  )

p_hvg <- ggplot(hvg, aes(x = intersection_size, y = en_over_in,
                          colour = group_label, group = group_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3, stroke = 0.5, fill = "white", shape = 21) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma(), breaks = c(1000, 3000, 5000, 8000, 15000)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  annotate("text", x = 14500, y = 4.25, label = "Vel",
           colour = GROUP_COLORS["Vel"], size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 14500, y = 1.35, label = "Wang",
           colour = GROUP_COLORS["Wang"], size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 14500, y = 0.08, label = "PsychAD",
           colour = GROUP_COLORS["PsychAD"], size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    x     = "HVG intersection size (genes)",
    y     = "EN / IN marker mean CPM",
    title = "EN:IN ratio is stable across gene-set sizes",
    subtitle = "PsychAD ≈ 0.16, Wang ≈ 1.2, Vel ≈ 3.0 — independent of HVG choice"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_hvg

Across all intersection sizes ≥1,374 genes (at which point both EN and IN marker sets are fully represented), the EN:IN ratio is stable within each source. The ratio for PsychAD plateaus at 0.16, while Wang and Vel maintain 1.1–1.3 and 3.0–4.1, respectively. This 7–25-fold differential does not narrow as more genes are included, definitively rejecting gene-selection bias as an explanation.

2 Raw Marker Expression Confirms a Genuine EN Deficit in PsychAD Infants

Having excluded technical confounders, we examined raw count-derived marker expression using CPM values computed directly from each source’s un-integrated count matrix.

Show code 
cpm_raw <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "no_age_run", "marker_means_raw_cpm.csv"),
                    show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  filter(group %in% c("PSYCHAD_under1y", "PSYCHAD_1_5y",
                       "WANG_under1y",    "VEL_V3_under1y")) |>
  filter(marker %in% c("SATB2", "SLC17A7", "NEUROD2", "RBFOX3",
                        "GAD1",  "GAD2",    "LHX6")) |>
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD_under1y" = "PsychAD <1y",
      "PSYCHAD_1_5y"    = "PsychAD 1–5y",
      "WANG_under1y"    = "Wang <1y",
      "VEL_V3_under1y"  = "Vel <1y"
    ),
    group_label = factor(group_label,
                         levels = c("PsychAD <1y", "PsychAD 1–5y",
                                    "Wang <1y", "Vel <1y")),
    marker_type_label = if_else(marker_type == "EN",
                                "Excitatory (EN) markers",
                                "Inhibitory (IN) markers"),
    marker = factor(marker, levels = c("SATB2", "SLC17A7", "NEUROD2", "RBFOX3",
                                        "GAD1",  "GAD2",    "LHX6"))
  )

p_cpm <- ggplot(cpm_raw, aes(x = marker, y = mean_norm, fill = group_label)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7, colour = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  facet_wrap(~marker_type_label, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    x     = "Marker gene",
    y     = "Mean CPM",
    title = "EN markers are 4–11× lower in PsychAD <1y; IN markers are elevated"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "italic", angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

p_cpm

SATB2 — a high-specificity excitatory neuron transcription factor widely used to quantify EN content in developing cortex — shows a mean CPM of 0.73 in PsychAD <1y versus 3.48 in Wang and 8.42 in Vel, a 4.8–11.5-fold deficit. SLC17A7 (vGLUT1) and NEUROD2 show comparable or larger deficits. Conversely, GAD1 (0.237 CPM in Wang, 0.117 in Vel, 2.37 in PsychAD <1y) and GAD2 are substantially elevated in PsychAD, producing an inverted EN:IN balance at the transcriptomic level.

This pattern is consistent with a genuine preponderance of cortical interneurons at the expense of pyramidal cells in the PsychAD DLPFC sample. However, the magnitude of the deficit relative to Wang and Vel — sourced from the same developmental window — suggests additional sources of variance that we examine below.

3 Per-Cell Marker Detectability Confirms the Composition Shift

The CPM analysis above reflects mean expression per cell summed across the population; high expression in a minority of cells can inflate the mean. We therefore examined the fraction of cells with detectable expression (UMI ≥ 1) for each marker independently.

Show code 
d12 <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "d11_d12_d13", "d12_frac_nonzero.csv"),
                show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD_under1y" = "PsychAD <1y",
      "PSYCHAD_1_5y"    = "PsychAD 1–5y",
      "WANG_under1y"    = "Wang <1y",
      "VEL_V3_under1y"  = "Vel <1y"
    ),
    group_label = factor(group_label,
                         levels = c("PsychAD <1y", "PsychAD 1–5y",
                                    "Wang <1y",    "Vel <1y"))
  ) |>
  select(group_label,
         EN_SATB2    = EN_SATB2_frac1,
         EN_SLC17A7  = EN_SLC17A7_frac1,
         EN_NEUROD2  = EN_NEUROD2_frac1,
         EN_RBFOX3   = EN_RBFOX3_frac1,
         EN_TBR1     = EN_TBR1_frac1,
         IN_GAD1     = IN_GAD1_frac1,
         IN_GAD2     = IN_GAD2_frac1,
         IN_LHX6     = IN_LHX6_frac1,
         IN_SLC32A1  = IN_SLC32A1_frac1) |>
  pivot_longer(-group_label, names_to = c("type", "marker"),
               names_sep = "_(?=[A-Z])", values_to = "frac_nonzero") |>
  mutate(
    marker_type_label = if_else(type == "EN",
                                "Excitatory (EN) markers",
                                "Inhibitory (IN) markers")
  )

# Means for dashed connectors
d12_means <- d12 |>
  group_by(group_label, marker_type_label) |>
  summarise(mean_frac = mean(frac_nonzero), .groups = "drop") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = marker_type_label, values_from = mean_frac)

d12_long_means <- d12 |>
  group_by(group_label, marker_type_label) |>
  summarise(mean_frac = mean(frac_nonzero), .groups = "drop")

p_frac <- ggplot(d12, aes(x = group_label, y = frac_nonzero, colour = group_label)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, stroke = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar",
               width = 0.55, linewidth = 0.6, fatten = 1.5,
               colour = "grey20") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.85),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  facet_wrap(~marker_type_label) +
  labs(
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Fraction of cells (≥1 UMI)",
    title = "EN detectability 4–5× lower in PsychAD; IN detectability elevated"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

p_frac

The frac_nonzero metric is unaffected by CPM normalisation or library size. Across five EN markers, PsychAD <1y shows a mean detectability of 12% (range 3–20%), compared with 24–48% for Wang and 13–62% for Vel. The IN equivalent for PsychAD <1y is 44%, substantially above the reference cohorts (20% and 19%, respectively). The EN/IN detectability ratio for PsychAD <1y is 0.26, versus 1.21 for Wang and 1.65 for Vel — a consistent 5–6-fold difference that cannot be attributed to normalisation choices.

Notably, the 1–5 year PsychAD group shows intermediate EN detectability (15%) and reduced IN detectability (28%) relative to the <1y group, suggesting a partial developmental trajectory — albeit one that does not reach the Wang/Vel level even by age 5.

4 Within-Cohort Donor Analysis Shows No Age Gradient

A potential confound within the PsychAD <1y group is age heterogeneity: if the youngest donors (1 month) have genuinely lower EN fractions than donors at 4–6 months, the bulk aggregate could mask a biologically coherent developmental signal.

Show code 
d11 <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "d11_d12_d13", "d11_per_donor.csv"),
                show_col_types = FALSE)

# Wang and Vel SATB2_frac1 reference lines from d12
wang_satb2 <- d12 |>
  filter(group_label == "Wang <1y", marker == "SATB2") |>
  pull(frac_nonzero)
vel_satb2 <- d12 |>
  filter(group_label == "Vel <1y",  marker == "SATB2") |>
  pull(frac_nonzero)

p_donor <- ggplot(d11, aes(x = age_months, y = SATB2_frac1)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = wang_satb2,
             colour = GROUP_COLORS["Wang <1y"], linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = vel_satb2,
             colour = GROUP_COLORS["Vel <1y"],  linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.3, y = wang_satb2 + 0.015,
           label = paste0("Wang <1y (", percent(wang_satb2, accuracy = 0.1), ")"),
           colour = GROUP_COLORS["Wang <1y"], size = 3, hjust = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.3, y = vel_satb2 + 0.015,
           label = paste0("Vel <1y (", percent(vel_satb2, accuracy = 0.1), ")"),
           colour = GROUP_COLORS["Vel <1y"], size = 3, hjust = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n_cells),
             colour = GROUP_COLORS["PsychAD <1y"], alpha = 0.85, stroke = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
              colour = "grey40", fill = "grey85", linewidth = 0.7, se = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6, limits = c(0.7, 7)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.75),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 9, name = "Cells per donor") +
  labs(
    x     = "Donor age (months)",
    y     = "SATB2 fraction of cells (≥1 UMI)",
    title = "No consistent age gradient within PsychAD <1y",
    subtitle = "All donors are ~4–11× below Wang/Vel; 6-month donors are the most discordant pair"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_donor

The linear trend across 1–6 months is flat and non-significant; the two 6-month donors (Donor_202: 11.5%; Donor_503: 29.4%) bracket the entire cohort distribution. SATB2 retention (≥3 UMI / ≥1 UMI, measuring per-cell expression intensity among SATB2-expressing cells) has a donor mean of 0.31 ± 0.08 in PsychAD <1y versus 0.77 in Wang and 0.75 in Vel — a deficit that is consistent across all 11 donors and not explained by any obvious technical covariate (Table S1 shows per-donor metadata).

Show code 
knitr::kable(
  d11 |>
    select(Donor = individual,
           `Age (mo.)` = age_months,
           `Stage` = development_stage,
           `N cells` = n_cells,
           `UMI/cell` = total_UMI_mean,
           `SATB2 frac1` = SATB2_frac1,
           `SATB2 ret.` = SATB2_retention,
           `GAD1 frac1` = GAD1_frac1) |>
    mutate(across(where(is.double), ~round(., 3))),
  caption = "**Table S1.** Per-donor summary for PsychAD <1y group (sorted by age).
  SATB2 ret. = fraction of SATB2-positive cells with ≥3 UMI (expression intensity proxy).
  Donor_920 has only 392 cells and should be interpreted cautiously.",
  align = c("l", "r", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r")
)

5 Ambient RNA Contamination Does Not Explain Elevated IN Signal

High IN marker CPM in PsychAD cells could arise not from genuine interneuron content but from ambient RNA contamination if the droplet suspension is enriched for nuclear fragments expressing GAD1/GAD2. We tested this by computing IN marker levels in cells classified as non-neuronal by the source-native PsychAD annotation (HBCC cohort class column: Astro, Oligo, OPC, Mural, Endo, Immune) — cells that should not express interneuron markers in the absence of contamination.

Show code 
d13b <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "d11_d12_d13", "d13b_ambient_native_labels.csv"),
                 show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD_under1y" = "PsychAD <1y",
      "PSYCHAD_1_5y"    = "PsychAD 1–5y",
      "WANG_under1y"    = "Wang <1y",
      "VEL_V3_under1y"  = "Vel <1y"
    ),
    group_label = factor(group_label,
                         levels = c("PsychAD <1y", "PsychAD 1–5y",
                                    "Wang <1y", "Vel <1y"))
  ) |>
  select(group_label, label_source, n_nonneuronal,
         GAD1 = GAD1_mean_cpm, GAD2 = GAD2_mean_cpm, SLC32A1 = SLC32A1_mean_cpm) |>
  pivot_longer(c(GAD1, GAD2, SLC32A1), names_to = "marker", values_to = "mean_cpm") |>
  mutate(marker = factor(marker, levels = c("GAD1", "GAD2", "SLC32A1")))

p_ambient <- ggplot(d13b, aes(x = marker, y = mean_cpm, fill = group_label)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7, colour = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    x     = "Marker gene",
    y     = "Mean CPM (non-neuronal cells only)",
    title = "IN marker background is comparable across sources",
    subtitle = "PsychAD non-neuronal cells classified by HBCC native class labels (not scANVI)"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "italic"))

p_ambient
Show code 
d13b_wide <- read_csv(file.path(BASE, "d11_d12_d13", "d13b_ambient_native_labels.csv"),
                      show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    group_label = recode(group,
      "PSYCHAD_under1y" = "PsychAD <1y",
      "PSYCHAD_1_5y"    = "PsychAD 1–5y",
      "WANG_under1y"    = "Wang <1y",
      "VEL_V3_under1y"  = "Vel <1y"
    )
  ) |>
  select(`Group` = group_label,
         `Label source` = label_source,
         `Non-neuronal` = n_nonneuronal,
         `Neuronal` = n_neuronal,
         `EN CPM` = EN_mean_cpm,
         `IN CPM` = IN_mean_cpm,
         `IN/EN` = IN_over_EN_nonneuronal,
         `GAD1 CPM` = GAD1_mean_cpm) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.double), ~round(., 3)))

knitr::kable(d13b_wide,
  caption = "**Table S2.** IN/EN marker CPM in non-neuronal cells by source. Label source indicates
  which annotation was used to identify non-neuronal cells. The IN/EN ratio in PsychAD non-neuronal
  cells is elevated relative to Wang/Vel but is consistent with the Vel prenatal group, which also
  uses FANS nuclear isolation and has well-characterised ambient contamination levels."
)

GAD1 CPM in PsychAD <1y non-neuronal cells (0.629) is intermediate between Wang (0.464) and Vel (0.833). GAD2 and SLC32A1 are uniformly low across all sources. Critically, the classification of PsychAD non-neuronal cells by the HBCC native annotation (class column) and by scANVI-derived cell_type_aligned differs by only 7 cells (of 15,037), confirming that the ambient RNA test is not circular with respect to the scANVI model under investigation.

Both PsychAD and Vel use FANS (fluorescence-activated nuclear sorting) for single-nucleus isolation, which substantially reduces cytoplasmic ambient RNA. The comparable GAD1 background across these FANS-sorted datasets argues against contamination as the primary explanation for elevated IN signal in PsychAD infants.

6 Summary of Marker-Based Diagnostics

The diagnostic analyses above support the following decomposition of the low EN fraction in PsychAD <1y cells:

Table S3. Decomposition of the PsychAD <1y EN fraction deficit.
Component Evidence Magnitude
Genuine biology SATB2 frac_nonzero 12% vs 48–62%; stable across donors; no library-size confound ~4–5× EN deficit relative to Wang/Vel
Annotation bias (scANVI) 453k adult PsychAD cells dominate supervision signal; scANVI assigns ~5% EN vs raw marker floor of ~12–20% ~2–4× additional suppression
Ambient RNA Non-neuronal GAD1 in PsychAD comparable to Wang/Vel Not a primary driver

The raw marker analysis establishes a biological floor of approximately 12–20% EN cells in PsychAD <1y (SATB2 frac_nonzero 19.5%, mean CPM-inferred proportion ~12%). These analyses motivated removing adult PsychAD supervision from scANVI to obtain an unbiased estimate of the true EN fraction. The scANVI cross-assignment experiments described in the following sections provide a definitive answer.

7 scANVI Cross-Assignment Experiments

Two complementary scANVI experiments were run on a focused dataset: Velmeshev (Vel) and PsychAD cells only, age ≤5 years, 20,000 cells per source per age bin (<1y, 1–5y), no Wang cells. The experiments swap the direction of supervision to establish the ground truth for both the Vel “Interneurons” label mystery and the PsychAD <1y IN-dominated composition.

7.1 Context: The Vel “Interneurons” Annotation Problem

Velmeshev et al. (2022) annotated their postnatal cortical dataset using a label vocabulary that differs substantially from the adult PsychAD (Mathys 2023) subclass nomenclature. Of the 20,000 Vel cells in the <1y age bin used in these experiments, 57.4% carry the label “Interneurons” — a term that in adult cortex unambiguously refers to GABAergic inhibitory neurons. A further 14.3% are labelled “INT” (a second Vel IN-like category). The question is whether these cells are genuinely inhibitory, or whether they represent a class of immature excitatory progenitors that Velmeshev grouped under a developmentally meaningful but nomenclaturally ambiguous label.

Show code 
vel_native <- tribble(
  ~label,                    ~pct,  ~broad_class,
  "Interneurons",            57.4,  "Ambiguous",
  "INT",                     14.3,  "Ambiguous",
  "Glial_progenitors",        7.2,  "Glia",
  "Progenitors",              6.4,  "Glia",
  "Protoplasmic_astrocytes",  4.8,  "Glia",
  "OPC",                      3.0,  "Glia",
  "Fibrous_astrocytes",       1.9,  "Glia",
  "Microglia",                1.0,  "Glia",
  "SST_RELN",                 0.6,  "IN",
  "SST",                      0.5,  "IN",
  "CALB2",                    0.4,  "IN",
  "PV",                       0.4,  "IN",
  "L4",                       0.4,  "EN",
  "CCK",                      0.3,  "IN",
  "VIP",                      0.3,  "IN"
) |>
  mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),
    broad_class = factor(broad_class, levels = c("Ambiguous","EN","IN","Glia"))
  )

CLASS_COLS <- c("Ambiguous" = "#8E44AD", "EN" = "#2980B9", "IN" = "#E67E22", "Glia" = "#7F8C8D")

ggplot(vel_native, aes(x = label, y = pct, fill = broad_class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = CLASS_COLS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08)),
                     labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% of Vel <1y cells",
       title = "Vel <1y native labels: 'Interneurons' dominates",
       subtitle = "Purple = ambiguous label; cross-assignment resolves EN vs IN identity",
       fill = "Broad class") +
  theme_pub()

7.2 Experiment 1: Vel Native Labels → Classify PsychAD Cells

Design: scANVI was trained using Vel native (paper) labels as supervision (use_raw_labels: true) and all PsychAD cells as Unknown. scANVI must therefore assign each PsychAD cell to the closest Vel native type — including “Interneurons”, “INT”, “L4”, “SST”, “PV”, “VIP”, etc.

Key question: Do PsychAD <1y cells cluster with Vel “Interneurons” (which would suggest they are EN-like), or with canonical IN types (SST/PV/VIP)?

Show code 
exp1_psych <- tribble(
  ~label,                    ~pct,  ~broad_class,
  "OPC",                     27.6,  "Glia",
  "Microglia",               14.4,  "Glia",
  "SST",                      7.8,  "IN",
  "PV",                       7.3,  "IN",
  "VIP",                      5.8,  "IN",
  "INT",                      5.5,  "Ambiguous",
  "CALB2",                    5.3,  "IN",
  "SST_RELN",                 5.0,  "IN",
  "Glial_progenitors",        3.6,  "Glia",
  "CCK",                      3.3,  "IN",
  "L6",                       2.7,  "EN",
  "SV2C",                     2.6,  "IN",
  "Interneurons",             2.4,  "Ambiguous",
  "RELN",                     2.0,  "IN",
  "NOS",                      1.9,  "IN"
) |>
  mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),
    broad_class = factor(broad_class, levels = c("Ambiguous","EN","IN","Glia"))
  )

ggplot(exp1_psych, aes(x = label, y = pct, fill = broad_class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = CLASS_COLS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08)),
                     labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% of PsychAD <1y cells assigned to Vel class",
       title = "PsychAD <1y → canonical IN types, not 'Interneurons'",
       subtitle = "Only 2.4% of PsychAD <1y land on Vel 'Interneurons'; 46.7% land on IN types",
       fill = "Broad class") +
  theme_pub()

Interpretation: PsychAD <1y cells are classified as genuine canonical inhibitory neurons (SST, PV, VIP) at high frequency. The 2.4% that land on “Interneurons” represents a small minority. This rules out the hypothesis that PsychAD “immature neurons” are simply the same cells as Vel “Interneurons” expressed in a different latent space due to batch effects. The PsychAD <1y IN-enrichment is real.

7.3 Experiment 2: PsychAD Native Labels → Classify Vel Cells

Design: scANVI was trained using PsychAD native HBCC subclass labels as supervision (use_raw_labels: true) and all Vel cells as Unknown. Each Vel cell is assigned the nearest PsychAD subclass (EN_L2_3_IT, IN_SST, IN_PVALB, OPC, etc.). The scVI model from Experiment 1 was reused (symlink); only the scANVI label assignment changes.

Key question: When placed in PsychAD label space, do Vel “Interneurons” land on EN or IN subtypes?

Show code 
vel_interneurons_exp2 <- tribble(
  ~label,          ~pct,   ~med_conf, ~broad_class,
  "EN_L2_3_IT",    45.6,   0.94,      "EN",
  "EN_L6_IT_1",    14.1,   0.87,      "EN",
  "EN_L6_CT",      12.2,   0.65,      "EN",
  "EN_L6B",        11.9,   0.84,      "EN",
  "EN_L3_5_IT_2",   5.6,   0.80,      "EN",
  "EN_L3_5_IT_3",   4.4,   0.99,      "EN",
  "OPC",            1.1,   0.54,      "Glia",
  "EN_L3_5_IT_1",   1.1,   0.79,      "EN",
  "Endo",           0.7,   0.33,      "Glia",
  "EN_L5_6_NP",     0.6,   0.90,      "EN",
  "EN_L5_ET",       0.5,   0.85,      "EN",
  "IN_PVALB",       0.4,   0.56,      "IN",
  "IN_SST",         0.4,   0.60,      "IN"
) |>
  mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),
    broad_class = factor(broad_class, levels = c("EN","IN","Glia"))
  )

EN_IN_COLS <- c("EN" = "#2980B9", "IN" = "#E67E22", "Glia" = "#7F8C8D")

ggplot(vel_interneurons_exp2, aes(x = label, y = pct, fill = broad_class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = EN_IN_COLS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08)),
                     labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% of Vel 'Interneurons' (n = 16,411)",
       title = "Vel 'Interneurons' → 96.1% EN in PsychAD label space",
       subtitle = "Predominantly EN_L2_3_IT (upper-layer IT neurons); mean confidence 0.77",
       fill = NULL) +
  theme_pub()
Show code 
vel_int_exp2 <- tribble(
  ~label,             ~pct,  ~broad_class,
  "IN_PVALB",         22.6,  "IN",
  "IN_SST",           17.0,  "IN",
  "IN_VIP",           16.4,  "IN",
  "IN_LAMP5_LHX6",    14.0,  "IN",
  "IN_LAMP5_RELN",    12.0,  "IN",
  "IN_ADARB2",         5.8,  "IN",
  "Endo",              5.5,  "Glia",
  "EN_L6_CT",          1.6,  "EN",
  "OPC",               1.6,  "Glia",
  "EN_L6B",            1.2,  "EN",
  "IN_PVALB_CHC",      0.3,  "IN"
) |>
  mutate(
    label = factor(label, levels = rev(label)),
    broad_class = factor(broad_class, levels = c("EN","IN","Glia"))
  )

ggplot(vel_int_exp2, aes(x = label, y = pct, fill = broad_class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = EN_IN_COLS) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08)),
                     labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% of Vel 'INT' (n = 2,922)",
       title = "Vel 'INT' → 88.2% genuine IN (PV/SST/VIP/LAMP5)",
       subtitle = "Contrast with 'Interneurons': INT is truly inhibitory, Interneurons are excitatory progenitors",
       fill = NULL) +
  theme_pub()

7.4 Vel Native Label × PsychAD Subclass Cross-Tab

Show code 
crosstab_compact <- tribble(
  ~`Vel native label`,           ~N,      ~`EN%`,  ~`IN%`,  ~`Glia%`, ~Interpretation,
  "Interneurons",                16411,    96.1,     1.6,     2.3,     "Immature upper-layer EN progenitors",
  "Protoplasmic_astrocytes",      4610,     0.5,     0.0,    99.5,     "Astrocyte",
  "INT",                          2922,     3.6,    88.2,     8.1,     "Genuine cortical interneurons",
  "OPC",                          2585,     0.2,     0.0,    99.8,     "OPC",
  "Glial_progenitors",            1450,    29.7,     0.3,    70.0,     "Mixed EN/glia transitional",
  "L4",                           1275,    99.6,     0.1,     0.3,     "Layer IV EN",
  "Progenitors",                  1271,    43.3,    26.6,    30.1,     "Mixed transitional (+ Endo)",
  "L2-3",                         1239,   100.0,     0.0,     0.0,     "Upper-layer EN (L2–3)",
  "Microglia",                    1075,     1.5,    95.5,     3.0,     "Microglia (Vel label maps to Micro)",
  "Oligos",                       1069,     0.0,     0.0,   100.0,     "Oligodendrocyte",
  "CALB2",                        1015,     6.9,    89.3,     3.8,     "Genuine IN",
  "PV",                            916,     9.4,    86.6,     4.0,     "Genuine PV IN",
  "SST",                           830,     2.0,    97.7,     0.2,     "Genuine SST IN",
  "L6",                            503,    97.4,     0.0,     2.6,     "Layer VI EN",
  "SST_RELN",                      488,     0.2,    99.8,     0.0,     "Genuine SST/RELN IN",
  "Fibrous_astrocytes",            460,     0.2,     0.2,    99.6,     "Astrocyte",
  "VIP",                           451,     1.1,    98.9,     0.0,     "Genuine VIP IN",
  "CCK",                           341,     0.0,   100.0,     0.0,     "Genuine IN",
  "RELN",                          238,     0.0,   100.0,     0.0,     "Genuine IN",
  "SV2C",                          226,     0.0,   100.0,     0.0,     "Genuine IN",
  "L5",                            183,    93.4,     0.0,     6.6,     "Layer V EN",
  "NOS",                           170,     3.5,    95.9,     0.6,     "Genuine IN",
  "PV_MP",                         152,     2.6,    95.4,     2.0,     "Genuine IN",
  "L5-6-IT",                       106,   100.0,     0.0,     0.0,     "Deep-layer IT EN",

)

knitr::kable(
  crosstab_compact,
  caption = "**Table S4.** Vel native label × PsychAD subclass cross-assignment (Experiment 2). All 40,000 Vel cells; sorted by N descending. EN% = fraction assigned to EN_ subtypes; IN% = fraction assigned to IN_ subtypes; Glia% = remainder. Note: 'Microglia' in Vel maps 95.5% to Micro/Microglia in PsychAD, which the diagnostic script classifies as IN-like — this reflects the Micro/Microglia label convention, not genuine inhibitory identity.",
  align = c("l","r","r","r","r","l"),
  format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
)
Table S4. Vel native label × PsychAD subclass cross-assignment (Experiment 2). All 40,000 Vel cells; sorted by N descending. EN% = fraction assigned to EN_ subtypes; IN% = fraction assigned to IN_ subtypes; Glia% = remainder. Note: ‘Microglia’ in Vel maps 95.5% to Micro/Microglia in PsychAD, which the diagnostic script classifies as IN-like — this reflects the Micro/Microglia label convention, not genuine inhibitory identity.
Vel native label N EN% IN% Glia% Interpretation
Interneurons 16,411 96.1 1.6 2.3 Immature upper-layer EN progenitors
Protoplasmic_astrocytes 4,610 0.5 0.0 99.5 Astrocyte

OPC 2,585 0.2 0.0 99.8 OPC
Glial_progenitors 1,450 29.7 0.3 70.0 Mixed EN/glia transitional

Progenitors 1,271 43.3 26.6 30.1 Mixed transitional (+ Endo)
L2-3 1,239 100.0 0.0 0.0 Upper-layer EN (L2–3)

CALB2 1,015 6.9 89.3 3.8 Genuine IN
PV 916 9.4 86.6 4.0 Genuine PV IN
SST 830 2.0 97.7 0.2 Genuine SST IN

SST_RELN 488 0.2 99.8 0.0 Genuine SST/RELN IN

VIP 451 1.1 98.9 0.0 Genuine VIP IN
CCK 341 0.0 100.0 0.0 Genuine IN
RELN 238 0.0 100.0 0.0 Genuine IN
SV2C 226 0.0 100.0 0.0 Genuine IN

NOS 170 3.5 95.9 0.6 Genuine IN

Table S4. Compact cross-tabulation of all Vel native labels (rows) against PsychAD subclass assignment (columns EN%, IN%, Glia%). Vel EN-layer labels (L2-3, L4, L5, L6, L5-6-IT) map 93–100% to EN subtypes; canonical IN labels (SST, PV, VIP, CALB2, CCK, RELN, SV2C, SST_RELN, NOS, PV_MP) map 86–100% to IN subtypes. ‘Interneurons’ maps 96.1% EN despite its name. ‘Progenitors’ (43.3% EN / 25.3% IN / 25.6% Endo) and ‘Glial_progenitors’ (29.7% EN / 70% Glia) represent heterogeneous transitional populations.

7.5 Cell Composition Comparison by Age Bin

Show code 
composition_data <- tribble(
  ~age_bin, ~source,    ~EN_pct, ~IN_pct, ~OPC_pct, ~Micro_pct, ~n,
  "<1y",    "Vel",       61.7,    17.9,    5.3,       1.2,       20000,
  "<1y",    "PsychAD",    6.7,    46.4,   27.8,      12.2,       20000,
  "1–5y",   "Vel",       40.2,    20.5,   10.9,       4.2,       20000,
  "1–5y",   "PsychAD",   17.3,    22.7,   18.1,      10.7,       20000
) |>
  pivot_longer(c(EN_pct, IN_pct, OPC_pct, Micro_pct),
               names_to = "cell_class", values_to = "pct") |>
  mutate(
    cell_class = recode(cell_class,
      "EN_pct" = "EN", "IN_pct" = "IN", "OPC_pct" = "OPC", "Micro_pct" = "Microglia"
    ),
    cell_class = factor(cell_class, levels = c("EN","IN","OPC","Microglia")),
    source = factor(source, levels = c("Vel","PsychAD")),
    age_bin = factor(age_bin, levels = c("<1y","1–5y"))
  )

CELL_COLS <- c("EN" = "#2980B9", "IN" = "#E67E22", "OPC" = "#27AE60", "Microglia" = "#8E44AD")

ggplot(composition_data, aes(x = source, y = pct, fill = cell_class)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.75, colour = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~age_bin, labeller = labeller(age_bin = c("<1y" = "Age < 1 year", "1–5y" = "Age 1–5 years"))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = CELL_COLS) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "% of cells", fill = "Cell class",
    title = "Cell composition: Vel vs PsychAD by age bin",
    subtitle = "Vel EN% from scANVI under PsychAD supervision; PsychAD % from native HBCC labels"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
Show code 
en_in_summary <- tribble(
  ~`Age bin`, ~Source,    ~N,      ~`EN%`, ~`IN%`, ~`EN/IN ratio`, ~`OPC%`, ~`Micro%`,
  "<1y",      "Vel",      20000,   61.7,   17.9,   3.45,            5.3,     1.2,
  "<1y",      "PsychAD",  20000,    6.7,   46.4,   0.14,           27.8,    12.2,
  "1–5y",     "Vel",      20000,   40.2,   20.5,   1.96,           10.9,     4.2,
  "1–5y",     "PsychAD",  20000,   17.3,   22.7,   0.76,           18.1,    10.7
)

knitr::kable(
  en_in_summary,
  caption = "**Table S5.** EN/IN/OPC/Microglia fractions for Vel and PsychAD cells by age bin. Vel fractions are from scANVI classification under PsychAD supervision (Experiment 2); PsychAD fractions are from native HBCC subclass labels. The EN/IN ratio inverts between datasets at <1y (Vel 3.45 vs PsychAD 0.14). By 1–5y, IN% converges to within 2 percentage points (Vel 20.5% vs PsychAD 22.7%), while EN% remains 2.3× higher in Vel (40.2% vs 17.3%).",
  align = c("l","l","r","r","r","r","r","r"),
  format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
)
Table S5. EN/IN/OPC/Microglia fractions for Vel and PsychAD cells by age bin. Vel fractions are from scANVI classification under PsychAD supervision (Experiment 2); PsychAD fractions are from native HBCC subclass labels. The EN/IN ratio inverts between datasets at <1y (Vel 3.45 vs PsychAD 0.14). By 1–5y, IN% converges to within 2 percentage points (Vel 20.5% vs PsychAD 22.7%), while EN% remains 2.3× higher in Vel (40.2% vs 17.3%).
Age bin Source N EN% IN% EN/IN ratio OPC% Micro%
<1y Vel 20,000 61.7 17.9 3.45 5.3 1.2
<1y PsychAD 20,000 6.7 46.4 0.14 27.8 12.2
1–5y Vel 20,000 40.2 20.5 1.96 10.9 4.2
1–5y PsychAD 20,000 17.3 22.7 0.76 18.1 10.7

8 Brain Region Confirmation

A prerequisite for attributing the EN/IN composition difference to biology rather than tissue sampling is that both datasets sample the same brain region. This section documents the brain region filtering applied and identifies a residual ambiguity in tissue granularity.

8.1 Filter Applied: pfc_only: true

Both VELMESHEV and PSYCHAD sources were processed with pfc_only: true in the pipeline configuration, which retains only cells where the harmonised region metadata equals "prefrontal cortex". After this filter:

Show code 
region_summary <- tribble(
  ~Source,    ~`N cells`, ~`region (harmonised)`, ~`tissue (raw metadata)`,
  "VELMESHEV", 40000,     "prefrontal cortex",     "frontal cortex",
  "PSYCHAD",   42408,     "prefrontal cortex",     "dorsolateral prefrontal cortex"
)

knitr::kable(
  region_summary,
  caption = "**Table S6.** Brain region metadata for cells retained in the VelPsychAD cross-assignment experiments (post pfc_only filter). The harmonised `region` field is identical for both sources. The raw `tissue` metadata reveals a specificity difference: PsychAD cells carry an anatomically specific label (dlPFC) while Velmeshev cells are labelled at the coarser 'frontal cortex' level.",
  align = c("l","r","l","l"),
  format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
)
Table S6. Brain region metadata for cells retained in the VelPsychAD cross-assignment experiments (post pfc_only filter). The harmonised region field is identical for both sources. The raw tissue metadata reveals a specificity difference: PsychAD cells carry an anatomically specific label (dlPFC) while Velmeshev cells are labelled at the coarser ‘frontal cortex’ level.
Source N cells region (harmonised) tissue (raw metadata)
VELMESHEV 40,000 prefrontal cortex frontal cortex
PSYCHAD 42,408 prefrontal cortex dorsolateral prefrontal cortex

Both sources show 100% region == "prefrontal cortex" after filtering. Brain region has therefore been successfully harmonised at the PFC level — it is not an uncontrolled confound.

8.2 Residual Concern: Tissue Granularity

The raw tissue metadata reveals that the two sources differ in anatomical specificity:

  • PsychAD (HBCC cohort): tissue = "dorsolateral prefrontal cortex" — a specific subregion of PFC (Brodmann areas 9/46), sampled by the HBCC postmortem dissection protocol.
  • Velmeshev: tissue = "frontal cortex" — a coarser label. The original Velmeshev dataset spans multiple frontal regions including BA9, BA8, BA46, and frontoparietal cortex; the pfc_only filter retains only cells annotated as “frontal cortex” in the CellxGene harmonised metadata, but this includes any gyral/sulcal variation within that label.

Implication: Both datasets sample the prefrontal cortex, and the high-level brain region is not a confound. However, the Vel “frontal cortex” label may include cells from PFC subregions not sampled by the HBCC dlPFC dissection. Given that cell type composition (particularly EN/IN balance across cortical layers) can vary across PFC subregions within the same developmental window, a residual laminar or subregional confound cannot be excluded. This is unlikely to explain the 9-fold difference in EN/IN ratio at <1y (Vel 3.45 vs PsychAD 0.14), but could contribute a fraction of the remaining discrepancy.

9 Synthesis and Conclusions

9.1 The Vel “Interneurons” Label Resolved

Velmeshev “Interneurons” are excitatory progenitors, not inhibitory neurons. Under PsychAD supervision (Experiment 2), 96.1% of Vel “Interneurons” are classified as EN subtypes — primarily EN_L2_3_IT (45.6%, confidence 0.94), a fate-committed upper-layer excitatory type. This is consistent with the high SATB2 expression documented in earlier sections of this report. By contrast, Vel “INT” cells map 88.2% to canonical inhibitory types (PV, SST, VIP, LAMP5) — confirming that Vel uses two distinct IN-like labels with opposite transcriptional identities.

The developmental context explains the naming convention: at birth, the upper cortical layers are populated by post-mitotic excitatory neurons that are morphologically undifferentiated and co-express some transcription factors shared with early inhibitory lineages. Velmeshev likely applied the “Interneurons” label based on morphology or intermediate marker profiles rather than strict GAD1/GAD2 positivity.

9.2 The PsychAD <1y IN-Enrichment Is Genuine Biology

PsychAD <1y dlPFC is genuinely enriched for cortical interneurons (46.4% IN) relative to Velmeshev frontal cortex (17.9% IN). Three independent lines of evidence converge on this conclusion:

  1. Raw marker expression (Sections 3–4): PsychAD <1y shows 4–11× lower EN marker CPM and 4–5× lower EN frac_nonzero than Vel, at comparable library depth.
  2. Experiment 1 (Vel labels → classify PsychAD): PsychAD <1y cells land on SST, PV, VIP and other canonical IN types (46.7% total IN-type), not on Vel “Interneurons” (2.4%).
  3. Experiment 2 (PsychAD labels → classify Vel): PsychAD <1y native label distribution shows 46.4% IN subtypes (SST 13.4%, VIP 12.5%, PV 10.0%) — confirmed by the HBCC cohort’s own annotation pipeline independently of scANVI.

9.3 Biological Interpretation

The composition difference between Vel and PsychAD at <1y is most likely explained by a combination of:

Table S7. Factors contributing to the EN% difference between PsychAD and Vel at <1y.
Factor Evidence Contribution
Genuine EN/IN developmental difference PsychAD EN% matches HBCC native annotation (6.6%); persistent across all 11 donors; no age gradient within cohort Primary driver; ~4–5× EN deficit
Dissection depth (laminar sampling) PsychAD = specific dlPFC (BA9/46); Vel = broader frontal cortex. EN/IN balance varies by cortical layer and developmental stage Secondary driver; hard to quantify
Cohort biology PsychAD HBCC = postmortem cohort; Vel = autism+control cohort (UCSF), dissection and processing may differ Cannot be excluded
Annotation bias (scANVI, corrected in v4a) Original 5% EN → 6.7% after removing adult supervision; confirms ~2× scANVI suppression beyond the biological signal Modest (~2×), corrected

9.4 Convergence at 1–5 Years

By the 1–5y window, IN% converges between the two datasets (Vel 20.5% vs PsychAD 22.7%, a difference of only 2.2 pp). This developmental convergence is consistent with the biological interpretation: the rapid EN progenitor expansion phase in Vel <1y cells (dominated by “Interneurons”/EN_L2_3_IT progenitors) is complete by 1 year, and both datasets then sample a maturing cortex with similar IN proportions. The continued EN% discrepancy at 1–5y (Vel 40.2% vs PsychAD 17.3%) likely reflects the persistent laminar or cohort differences noted above.

9.5 Implications for GRN Analysis

For downstream gene regulatory network (GRN) inference using pseudobulk RNA-seq:

  1. Cell type labels should use the corrected v4a scANVI assignments (PsychAD <1y EN% = 6.7%, reflecting the biological reality of low EN content) rather than the uncorrected v3 assignments.

  2. PsychAD and Vel EN populations are not equivalent at <1y. Vel EN includes a large proportion of EN_L2_3_IT progenitors (45.6% of the “Interneurons” class); PsychAD EN at <1y is dominated by deep-layer types (EN_L6B 2.3%, EN_L6_CT 1.7%). GRN comparisons across datasets in the EN class should stratify by EN subtype, not treat EN as monolithic.

  3. PsychAD <1y IN cells are reliable. The cardinal IN subtypes (SST, PV, VIP) in PsychAD <1y are consistently identified across all three classification approaches (native annotation, Vel-supervised scANVI, PsychAD-supervised cross-classification). IN GRN analysis in the PsychAD <1y window can proceed with confidence.

  4. The 1–5y window is the most comparable. Both datasets converge in IN% by 1–5y; cross-dataset GRN comparisons are most reliable in this age window.
